title: resolve WritableStream locked error in agent streaming (#8302)

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(client-js): WritableStream locked error
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: Previous fix for same issue, was reverted in #8378

relatedIssues.title: [BUG] WritableStream is locked when using mastra/client-js inside nestjs service
relatedIssues.relationship: Fixes this issue. Issue was closed with a comment saying it was fixed in latest versions, but on main the setTimeout-based close pattern still exists.

summary: Fixes a race condition in client-js agent streaming where writable.close() was called while the stream was still locked by a concurrent pipeTo() operation, causing 'WritableStream is locked' errors in Node.js. This is the second attempt at fixing issue #8302 — the first fix (#8346) was merged then reverted (#8378). The approach uses pipeFinished/shouldCloseAfterPipe state flags to coordinate between the async pipeTo and onFinish callback, replacing the fragile setTimeout-based close pattern. Only modifies client-sdks/client-js/src/resources/agent.ts.
maintainerNotes: This PR has merge conflicts and needs rebasing. The underlying bug (setTimeout-based writable.close() racing with pipeTo lock) still appears to exist on main. Issue #8302 was closed with a maintainer saying it was fixed, but examining main shows the old setTimeout pattern is still in place — the first fix was reverted and no replacement landed. Greptile's automated review flagged a potential issue: the pipeFinished/shouldCloseAfterPipe flags are function-scoped, so recursive calls (client tool execution) create new flag instances, potentially causing double-close or unclosed streams. No tests or changeset included. The PR is 5 months old with conflicts — author needs to rebase, add tests for the recursive case, and add a changeset.
